name: Example A — The Kleptocrat
said: I serve the nation, not myself.
did: Personal wealth grew from $0 to estimated $70-200 billion during time in office.
Palace complex built at cost of $1.35 billion from public funds.
Family members placed in senior government and business positions (nepotism).
Jailed journalist who reported on corruption (censorship, destroyed evidence).
facts: Unexplained wealth orders filed in multiple jurisdictions.
Offshore accounts and shell companies linked to inner circle.
Opposition leaders jailed on vague charges.
Constitutional changes to extend power.
Media control — independent outlets shut down or taken over.
sources: Financial disclosure analysis by independent researchers

name: Example B — The War Starter
did: Invasion without UN authorization — war crime under international law.
Hundreds of thousands of civilian casualties documented by UN.
Cities turned to rubble — targeting civilian infrastructure.
Blocking humanitarian access to besieged areas.
Use of prohibited weapons documented by independent monitors.
facts: ICC arrest warrant issued for crimes against humanity.
Mass graves documented by satellite imagery.
Forced deportation of children documented by multiple organizations.
Torture confirmed by OHCHR reports.
Ethnic cleansing patterns identified by genocide scholars.

name: Example C — The CEO
said: Our employees are our greatest asset.
We care deeply about the environment.
did: Fired 12,000 workers while taking $40M bonus same quarter.
Internal memos show company knew about environmental damage since 1977.
Funded climate denial campaigns for 40 years (destroyed evidence of internal research).
Mandatory arbitration used to silence worker complaints about unsafe conditions.
Lobbied against minimum wage increase while company had record profits.
facts: SEC investigation for fraud in emissions reporting.
Wage theft class action — $380M settlement.
Supply chain audit found forced labor in 3 countries.
Tax evasion — effective rate 2.3% on $14B profit via offshore shell companies.
Whistleblower retaliation documented in court filings.

name: Example D — The Network
said: I have never met that person.
did: Flight logs show 26 trips to private island.
Sexual abuse of minors confirmed by federal conviction.
Trafficking children across state lines — federal charges.
NDAs used to silence victims for decades.
Network of powerful associates documented in court exhibits.
facts: Federal conviction for trafficking of minors.
Victim testimonies from 36+ individuals corroborating abuse pattern.
Institutional protection — first plea deal was non-prosecution agreement.
Associates include heads of state, royalty, CEOs — flight logs are court exhibits.
Cover-up by multiple institutions documented in federal investigation.
sources: Photographic evidence entered as court exhibits
